title: How Naming the Urge Out Loud Breaks Its Grip on a Godly Man
caption:
An urge kept in your head negotiates with you in private, and it always wins those negotiations. It only loses power the moment it has to answer to someone else.

Naming it isn't confession. It's interruption. You don't have to wait until you've already given in to say something. Text a brother the moment you feel it rising: "I'm fighting something right now." That sentence alone breaks the momentum.

The mechanism is embarrassment, and that's the point. Urges thrive in silence because silence costs nothing. The second you say it out loud, it costs you your pride, and pride was never yours to protect anyway.

Build the one-line habit this week. Pick your person. Pick your line. "Hey, I need to say something before I do it." Practice it on the small urges so it's ready for the big one.

#faith #christianmen #selfcontrol #godlymen #manofgod #discipline #accountability
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
